samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 2*size bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="size">Number of bits in the multiplier and multiplicand.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="mul_a_ext" value="'{size{1'b0}}' + mul_a"/>
        <assign id="mul_b_ext" value="'{size{1'b0}}' + mul_b"/>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Extending inputs
        wire [size-1:0] mul_a;
        wire [size-1:0] mul_b;
        wire [2*size-1:0] mul_out;
        
        // Intermediate variables
        reg [2*size-1:0] regs[0:size-1];
        integer i;
        // Generate partial products
        genvar j;
        generate
            for(j = 0; j < size; j=j+1) begin : gen_partial_products
                assign regs[j] = (mul_b[j]) ? (mul_a << j) : 'd0;
            end
        endgenerate
        
        // Add partial products
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                for(i = 0; i < size; i=i+1) begin
                    regs[i] <= 0;
                end
            end else begin
                for(i = 0; i < size; i=i+1) begin
                    regs[i] <= regs[i] + regs[(i+1)%size];
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Final product calculation
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                mul_out <= 0;
            end else begin
                mul_out <= regs[0];
            end
        end
    </implementation>
    
endmodule
